item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the definition of ‘short and sweet.’ The anomaly itself may feel somewhat underbaked, but the cultural touches give the article a nice layer of credibility and flavour. Overall a very Polished article.

i apologize

item.title: Re: It's a piece of cake to bake a pretty cake
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I had some trouble finding the balance in how much to say about the family. My MO is to go over-detailed, so I decided to be notably curt about them, give only hints toward their greater life and how the anomaly came to be.

item.title: Re: Nice
item.content:encoded:
In-universe, there's some basic computer script that auto-updates the document, and the Foundation is absolutely the sort of giant beureaucratic organisation that'd reprint the file when needed to keep physical copies up to date.

Out of universe, it's part of a running thing I've done in all my articles where the reader is not necessarily reading the article in the modern day, and I want to clue the reader toward the time period they're reading it in (in this case, early 2000s).

You're right about the class, though, thats a relic from the original draft. I'll chat with some others about it, probably change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! I've had this ready since Tuesday, but decided it'd be fun to wait.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a signature pączki with rose jam and orange rind

This may be the first time a skip has made me hungry. ;_; I need this.

item.title: Re: a pun about dough and making money idk
item.content:encoded:
Shoot, right:

Image from Wikimedia Images, licenced under CC0 Creative Commons. Link here.

item.title: It's a piece of cake to bake a pretty cake
item.content:encoded:
If the way is hazy.

It was a very fun read, and I haven't seen anything like it done before. The only thing that I really had an 'issue' with is that I was starting to get interested with the Moczydłowski family, but information regarding it was cut just a little short.

Still, not a big deal, just something I noticed.

Again, very well done. You have earned my upvote.

Keep up the good work, Rimple!

item.title: Absolutely Delicious
item.content:encoded: This was just a fun article to read, really wholesome and warm-hearted with a slight edge of tragedy near the end. Well made, well done. +1

item.title: Nice
item.content:encoded:
It's good. Have my upvote.

Critiques:

Remove the thing about the next ethics committee meeting from the containment procedures. The Foundation wouldn't go through and change this every time a new one is scheduled. They'd just say "…next ethics committee meeting." and let the reader figure out when that is.

Footnotes aren't necessary in my opinion, but you might've been going for something that I don't see.

Why's it Euclid? Seems relatively easy to contain, even beneficial.

item.title: Re: a pun about dough and making money idk
item.content:encoded: You've done more with this than I could've possibly hoped for, and I applaud you. Very well done.

item.title: a pun about dough and making money idk
item.content:encoded:
Ayyyyyyyyyyy.

It's been over a year since I put out a solo skip. IRL is garbage, blah blah. This was written as part of the Draft Swap, a little project I'm organising. Fingers crossed you'll be hearing more about that in the future. As part of that, the concept and first half-draft of this were written by MaliceAF, and then I came in and turned it into a working article. All going well, you should be seeing a couple articles like that in the next week or two. It was really fun to work like this, finding the balance of keeping my own style while maintaining the tone and intent of the original idea.

Thanks to the dozen people who helped out with this, particularly Perelka_L for some Polish accuracy (including the name of the restaurant), minmin for nicely detailed last-minute crit, and my Dad for helping me figure out some of the bureaucratic shit in the conprocs. <3 you all.
